methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
General Galactic is a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Halen Mattison and Luke Neise that has raised $10M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (Graphyte, Fourth Power, and Steady Energy) valued between $110M and $200M, supplemented by reference to a broader frontier-energy comparable universe of 9 private firms and 1 public entity.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $25M based on total raised as a proxy for current valuation. Peer-median analysis benchmarks against the three comparable companies, which trade at a median valuation of $150M, implying General Galactic could reach similar levels as it advances. These methods produce a range from $25M to $150M.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $21M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $88M. The substantial spread reflects General Galactic's current Seed stage and the inherent distance between early-stage financing and Series A/later valuations typical of the peer set. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the crowded frontier-energy space, capital efficiency relative to peers, and market timing for advancement to later funding stages.
